stem: A company had 400 employees at the start of the year. During the year, the number of employees increased by 15%, and then during the following year, the number decreased by 10%. How many employees did the company have at the end of the second year?

distractorRationale.B: Student added the percentages (15% − 10% = 5% net increase) and applied to the original: 400 × 1.05 = 420.
distractorRationale.C: Student assumed 15% up then 10% down returns to the original: 400.
distractorRationale.D: Student applied only the first increase and stopped: 400 × 1.15 = 460.
